The share transfer would take place in lieu of Vodafone injecting more cash into the Indian business, according to the people.

articles.title: Weekly Numerology Horoscope (11th May - 17th May, 2026): Which Life Path Numbers Will See the Biggest Success?
articles.description: Week 20 of 2026 arrives with a brighter, faster, and more action-oriented vibration compared to last week’s disciplined and heavy energy. If the previous week felt like a test of patience and responsibility, this week feels like a reward — the universe now opens doors for those who stayed consistent and didn’t give up.This week carries an energetic push for new beginnings, leadership, confidence, and forward movement. Many people will feel a strong urge to initiate something new — a fresh project, career decision, personal transformation, or even a lifestyle change. The energy supports fresh planning, bold execution, and quick progress, but it also demands smart thinking. The universe is saying: move forward, but move wisely.

articles.title: Twitch CEO Dan Clancy announces stricter action against viewbotting
articles.description: Twitch is implementing a new system to combat viewbotting, a practice where streamers artificially inflate viewer numbers. Starting in May, repeat offenders will face concurrent viewership caps based on their real traffic, with penalties escalating for persistent violations. The platform aims to create a fairer environment for legitimate creators by addressing fake engagement.

articles.title: Denon Home series speakers review: Siri smarts with superior sound
articles.description: Denon's new line of Siri-enabled Apple Home smart speakers may be what users are looking for in the absence of updated HomePod and HomePod mini. Let's take a listen. Denon Home series speakers review: These new smart speakers support Siri & Apple Home with premium audio Japanese audio brand Denon is out with its latest range of speakers : the Denon Home 200, Denon Home 400, and Denon Home 600. While all different sizes and price points, the entire line caters to Apple users with support for conversing with Siri and AirPlay . The new devices launch in what has been a prolonged pause in Apple's HomePod product cycle. The second-generation full-sized HomePod launched in 2023, and HomePod mini has gone even longer without an update, hitting shelves in 2020. Continue Reading on AppleInsider | Discuss on our Forums

articles.title: Updated iOS 26.5 & iPadOS 26.5 RC 2 builds tighten final release
articles.description: Apple has seeded a second round of release candidate builds of iOS 26.5 and iPadOS 26.5, bringing the updates a step closer to public release with a smaller round of fixes and feature additions. Liquid Glass in iOS 26 The RC builds arrived one week after the fourth betas . Release candidates are typically the final versions Apple ships publicly unless major bugs force additional revisions during last-minute testing. Build number 23F77 appears on Apple's developer portal for both iOS 26.5 and iPadOS 26.5, replacing the earlier 23F75 RC build released on May 4. Continue Reading on AppleInsider | Discuss on our Forums

articles.title: Apple and Intel’s chip-making deal: What does this mean for the chip industry? Which Apple devices could get Intel chips?
articles.description: Apple and Intel have reportedly reached a preliminary agreement for the chipmaker to produce components for Apple hardware. This marks a potential return to collaboration after Apple's successful transition to its own Apple Silicon. While specific products remain unclear, discussions suggest Intel might manufacture Apple's lowest-end M processors as early as 2027.

articles.title: Bokaro STPI project fails to take off despite land allotment in 2017
articles.description: The vision for a Software Technology Parks of India (STPI) center in Bokaro, which has been in the works for almost a decade, is stalling due to inadequate funding from the state government. Despite securing land in 2017 for a sprawling 55,000-square-foot facility, construction is still at a standstill.

articles.title: HomeKit Weekly: The Airversa QliQ brings a rechargeable Thread smart button to Apple Home
articles.description: Smart buttons have become incredibly common in the Apple Home ecosystem, but finding one that checks every single box is still surprisingly challenging at times. The new Airversa QliQ immediately caught my attention because it addresses two of my biggest complaints about most smart switches: it uses a Thread connection instead of Bluetooth, and it ditches disposable coin batteries for a rechargeable one. Let’s take a closer look at what it brings to the table and see if its premium hardware features justify the purchase. more...

articles.title: The Apple Silicon chip in your next device may be made by Intel
articles.description: Apple has reportedly reached a preliminary agreement that will see Intel become a chipmaking partner, helping reduce the company's reliance on TSMC for Mac chips and more. Intel could soon be making Apple's chips. Image credit: Intel Reports had earlier suggested that the two companies were discussing a deal that would help Apple diversify its supply chain. Now, the pair are closer than ever to manufacturing some of Apple's chips in the United States. According to a report Wall Street Journal on Friday , the two companies have been in discussions about the project for more than a year. Significant progress has been made in recent months, however. Continue Reading on AppleInsider | Discuss on our Forums

articles.title: Intel Reportedly Planning 4 New CPU Lines Through 2028: Here's What to Expect
articles.description: Intel is on track to launch Nova Lake desktop CPUs with up to 52 cores in 2026, with Razer Lake next year. Mobile and budget-oriented chip lines, Titan Lake and Moon Lake, will follow in 2028.
